title: pittosporum viscidum
intro: Pittosporum viscidum, also known as the sticky Pittosporum or New Zealand tobacco tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. Pittosporum viscidum is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
